evals.prompt: hey i'm interested in setting up some coding standards for this repository — we're a small team of 3 devs and things are starting to get inconsistent. can you help me put together a STANDARDS.md?
evals.expected_output: A STANDARDS.md file written to the project root that is grounded in the actual codebase, addresses specific inconsistencies, covers naming and async patterns, includes rationale for rules.

assertions.description: Document addresses the async/await vs .then() inconsistency — the codebase mixes both styles
Document addresses the mixed function/method naming (camelCase getAllUsers vs snake_case update_user vs PascalCase DeleteUser)
Document is grounded in the actual codebase — references Express, pg, or JWT specifically
At least some rules include a 'Why' or brief rationale explaining the reason for the rule

evals.prompt: we just started this project 2 months ago and i want to lock in standards before we bring on more engineers. can you look at what we have and help write up a STANDARDS.md? our main concern is keeping the component and file naming consistent
evals.expected_output: A STANDARDS.md written to disk that identifies naming inconsistencies in the codebase, establishes clear naming conventions, covers React-specific patterns.

assertions.description: Directly resolves the snake_case vs PascalCase file naming inconsistency (transaction_card.jsx and settings.jsx vs Navbar.jsx, Button.jsx)
Addresses component structure — export style (default vs named), component naming conventions
Mentions Zustand (the state management library in use)
Document is focused and actionable — not an exhaustive 50-rule manifesto

evals.prompt: i want to document our coding conventions. we already have ruff configured but there's no written standards doc anywhere. what conventions should we be following?
evals.expected_output: A STANDARDS.md written to disk that notes ruff is authoritative, addresses naming inconsistencies not caught by ruff, establishes testing expectations.

assertions.description: Document explicitly acknowledges ruff handles style/formatting — doesn't duplicate its rules
Flags or resolves Client.py (PascalCase) vs invoice.py (lowercase) filename inconsistency
Flags or resolves CreateInvoice (PascalCase) vs get_invoices/update_invoice (snake_case) route function naming
Establishes a testing expectation even though no tests exist yet
